title: fraxinus lanuginosa var. serrata
intro: Fraxinus lanuginosa var. serrata, also known as the ash-leaved or white ash, is a variety of the Fraxinus lanuginosa tree, native to the mountainous regions of Japan and China. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which are derived from its bark and leaves. The health benefits of Fraxinus lanuginosa var. serrata include its ability to alleviate fever, reduce pain and swelling, and treat various skin conditions. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
